evals.prompt: Define the brand voice for a B2B cybersecurity company called 'Fortis'. They want to project confidence, expertise, and trustworthiness without sounding cold or robotic.
evals.expected_output: Produces a brand voice definition with personality traits, tone descriptors, vocabulary guidance, and do/don't content examples aligned to the B2B cybersecurity context.

assertions.text: Defines 3–5 brand voice personality traits with descriptions (e.g., 'Authoritative but approachable') rather than generic adjectives
Includes a tone scale or tone descriptors showing how the voice adapts across contexts (marketing vs support vs crisis)
Provides concrete do/don't language examples for each personality trait
Includes vocabulary guidance: preferred words, words to avoid, and industry jargon policy
Addresses the B2B cybersecurity context specifically — not generic brand voice advice

evals.prompt: Create a complete style guide for a wellness brand called 'Bloom'. Colors are sage green (#6B8F71), warm cream (#F5F0E8), and terracotta (#C06B4E). Font is Playfair Display for headings and Lato for body.
evals.expected_output: Assembles a comprehensive style guide covering color system, typography rules, logo usage, photography direction, and messaging tone with all provided values correctly applied.

assertions.text: Incorporates all three provided brand colors with correct hex values and adds neutral/text color recommendations
Defines typography hierarchy using Playfair Display for headings and Lato for body with size and weight specifications
Includes logo usage rules: clear space, minimum size, approved color variations, and incorrect usage examples
Provides photography and imagery direction aligned to the wellness brand personality
Assembles all elements into a shareable style guide structure with a clear table of contents

evals.prompt: Conduct a brand audit for our SaaS company. Our website uses 4 different shades of blue, 3 different fonts, and our LinkedIn posts don't match our website tone.
evals.expected_output: Performs a structured brand audit identifying inconsistencies, scoring brand cohesion, and providing a prioritized remediation plan.

assertions.text: Documents all identified inconsistencies: color variations, font proliferation, and tone misalignment
Scores or rates brand cohesion across the identified channels (website, social, communications)
Provides a prioritized remediation plan ordered by impact and effort
Recommends a single canonical color, a defined font hierarchy, and a tone-of-voice standard to resolve each inconsistency
Suggests a governance process or style guide update to prevent future drift

evals.prompt: Create a messaging framework for a startup launching an AI productivity tool for remote teams. Include positioning statement, value propositions, and tagline options.
evals.expected_output: Produces a complete messaging framework with a positioning statement template, tiered value propositions, audience-specific messaging, and 3–5 tagline options.

assertions.text: Provides a positioning statement following a recognized template (e.g., 'For [audience], [product] is the [category] that [key benefit] because [reason to believe]')
Includes 3–5 distinct value propositions mapped to specific audience pain points for remote teams
Offers at least 3 tagline options with rationale for each
Differentiates messaging by audience segment (e.g., individual contributor vs team lead vs executive buyer)
Avoids overused startup clichés (e.g., 'disruptive', 'game-changing', 'seamless') in favor of specific, credible language

evals.prompt: Define the visual identity standards for our logo. We have a primary logo, a horizontal variant, and a symbol/icon mark. How should each be used?
evals.expected_output: Produces visual identity standards for all three logo variants covering correct usage, clear space rules, minimum sizes, color variations, and incorrect usage examples.

assertions.text: Defines clear space rules (minimum padding) for all three logo variants using the logo's own unit as the measurement reference
Specifies minimum display sizes for print (mm) and screen (px) to ensure legibility
Defines approved color variations: full color, reversed (white), single-color, and black-only
Provides concrete incorrect usage examples: stretching, recoloring, adding effects, placing on busy backgrounds
Explains when to use each variant: primary for formal, horizontal for header/nav, symbol for favicon/app icon
